volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 558 - Section 746)

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: καὶ τί βουλόμεσθά γʼ ἄλλο —
sections.translation: What more can I desire —

sections.label: Section 558b
sections.original: νῦν ὁρᾷς ἃ χρή σʼ ὁρᾶν .
sections.translation: Thine eyes now open to the sights they should .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: ἢ Διὸς παιδὸς γενέσθαι παῖς ;
sections.translation: Than from a son of Zeus to spring ?

sections.label: Section 559b
sections.original: ὃ σοί γε γίγνεται .
sections.translation: Which is indeed thy lot .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἦ θίγω δῆθʼ οἵ μʼ ἔφυσαν ;
sections.translation: May I embrace the author of my being ?

sections.label: Section 560b
sections.original: πιθόμενός γε τῷ θεῷ .
sections.translation: Aye , put thy trust in the god .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: χαῖρέ μοι , πάτερ —
sections.translation: Hail to thee , father mine .

sections.label: Section 561b
sections.original: φίλον γε φθέγμʼ ἐδεξάμην τόδε .
sections.translation: With joy that title I accept .

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: ἡμέρα θʼ ἡ νῦν παροῦσα .
sections.translation: This day —

sections.label: Section 562b
sections.original: μακάριόν γʼ ἔθηκέ με .
sections.translation: Hath made me blest .

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: ὦ φίλη μῆτερ , πότʼ ἆρα καὶ σὸν ὄψομαι δέμας ;
sections.translation: Ah , mother dear ! shall I ever see thee too ? Now more than ever do I long to gaze upon thee , whoe ’ er thou art .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἴσως τέθνηκας , ἡμεῖς δʼ οὐδὲν ἂν δυναίμεθα .
sections.translation: But thou perhaps art dead , and I shall never have the chance .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: κοιναὶ μὲν ἡμῖν δωμάτων εὐπραξίαι ·
sections.translation: We share the good luck of thy house ; but still I could have wished my mistress too , and Erechtheus ’ line , had been blest with children .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ἐς μὲν σὴν ἀνεύρεσιν θεὸς
sections.translation: My son , albeit the god hath for thy discovery brought his oracle

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ὀρθῶς ἔκρανε , καὶ συνῆψʼ ἐμοί τε σέ ,
sections.translation: to a true issue , and united thee to me , while thou , too , hast found what most thou dost desire , till now unconscious of it ; still , as touching this anxiety so proper in thee , I feel an equal yearning that thou , my child , mayst find thy mother , and I the wife that bare thee unto me .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: χρόνῳ δὲ δόντες ταῦτʼ ἴσως εὕροιμεν ἄν .
sections.translation: Maybe we shall discover this , if we leave it to time . But now leave the courts of the god , and this homeless life of thine , and come to Athens , in accordance with thy fathers wishes , for there his happy realm and bounteous wealth await thee ; nor shalt thou

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: δυοῖν κεκλήσῃ δυσγενὴς πένης θʼ ἅμα ,
sections.translation: be taunted with base origin and poverty to boot , because in one of these respects thou something lackest , but thou shalt be renowned alike for birth and wealth . Art silent ? why dost fix thy eyes upon the ground ? Thou art lost in thought , and by this sudden change from thy former cheerfulness , thou strikest thy father with dismay .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: οὐ ταὐτὸν εἶδος φαίνεται τῶν πραγμάτων
sections.translation: Things assume a different form according as we see them before us , or far off . I am glad at what has happened , since I have found in thee a father ; but hear me on some points which I am now deciding .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: κλεινὰς Ἀθήνας οὐκ ἐπείσακτον γένος ,
sections.translation: Athens , I am told ,— that glorious city of a native race ,— owns no aliens ; in which case I shall force my entrance there under a twofold disadvantage , as an alien ’ s son and base-born as I am . Branded with this reproach , while as yet I am unsupported , I shall get the name of a mere nobody , a son of nobodies ;

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ἢν δʼ ἐς τὸ πρῶτον πόλεος ὁρμηθεὶς ζυγὸν
sections.translation: and if I win my way to the highest place in the state , and seek to be some one , I shall be hated by those who have no influence , for superiority is galling ; while ’ mongst men of worth who could show their wisdom , but are silent , and take no interest in politics ,

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: γέλωτʼ ἐν αὐτοῖς μωρίαν τε λήψομαι
sections.translation: I shall incur ridicule and be thought a fool for not keeping quiet in such a fault-finding city . Again , if I win a name amongst the men of mark who are engaged in politics , still more will jealous votes bar my progress ; for thus , father , is it ever wont to be ;

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: οἳ τὰς πόλεις ἔχουσι κἀξιώματα ,
sections.translation: they who have the city ’ s ear , and have already made their mark , are most bitter against all rivals .

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ἐλθὼν δʼ ἐς οἶκον ἀλλότριον ἔπηλυς ὢν
sections.translation: Again , if I , a stranger , come to a home that knows me not , and to that childless wife who before had thee as partner in her sorrow , but now

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: αὐτὴ καθʼ αὑτὴν τὴν τύχην οἴσει πικρῶς ,
sections.translation: will feel the bitterness of having to bear her fortune all alone ,— how , I ask , shall I not fairly earn her hatred , when I take my stand beside thee ; while she , still childless , sees thy dear pledge with bitter eyes ; and then thou have to choose between deserting me and regarding her ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἢ τἀμὰ τιμῶν δῶμα συγχέας ἔχῃς ;
sections.translation: or honouring me and utterly confounding thy home ? How many a murder , and death by deadly drugs have wives devised for husbands ! Besides , I pity that wife of thine , father , with her childless old age beginning ; she little deserves

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: πατέρων ἀπʼ ἐσθλῶν οὖσʼ ἀπαιδίᾳ νοσεῖν .
sections.translation: to pine in barrenness , a daughter of a noble race . That princely state we fondly praise is pleasant to the eye ; but yet in its mansions sorrow lurks ; for who is happy , or by fortune blest , that has to live his life in fear of violence with many a sidelong glance ?

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: αἰῶνα τείνει ; δημότης ἂν εὐτυχὴς
sections.translation: Rather would I live among the common folk , and taste their bliss , than be a tyrant who delights in making evil men his friends , and hates the good , in terror of his life . Perchance thou wilt tell me , Gold outweighs all these evils ,

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: πλουτεῖν τε τερπνόν ; οὐ φιλῶ ψόφους κλύειν
sections.translation: and wealth is sweet . I have no wish to be abused for holding tightly to my pelf , nor yet to have the trouble of it . Be mine a moderate fortune free from annoyance ! Now hear the blessings , father , that here were mine ; first , leisure , man ’ s chiefest joy ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ὄχλον τε μέτριον , οὐδέ μʼ ἐξέπληξʼ ὁδοῦ
sections.translation: with but moderate trouble ; no villain ever drove me from my path , and that is a grievance hard to bear , to make room and give way to sorry knaves . My duty was to pray unto the gods , or with mortal men converse , a minister to their joys , not to their Sorrows .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: καὶ τοὺς μὲν ἐξέπεμπον , οἳ δʼ ἧκον ξένοι ,
sections.translation: And I was ever dismissing one batch of guests , while another took their place , so that I was always welcome from the charm of novelty . That honesty which men must pray for , even against their will , custom and nature did conspire to plant in me in the sight of Phoebus . Now when I think on this ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: κρείσσω νομίζω τἀνθάδʼ ἢ τἀκεῖ , πάτερ .
sections.translation: I deem that I am better here than there , father . So let me live on here , for ’ tis an equal charm to joy in high estate , or in a humble fortune find a pleasure .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας , εἴπερ οὓς ἐγὼ φιλῶ
sections.translation: Well said ! if only those I love find their happiness in thy statement of the case .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: παῦσαι λόγων τῶνδʼ , εὐτυχεῖν δʼ ἐπίστασο ·
sections.translation: Cease such idle talk , and learn to be happy ; for on that spot where I discovered thee , my son , will I begin the rites , since I have chanced on the general banquet , open to all comers , and I will offer thy birth sacrifice which aforetime I left undone . And now will I bring thee to the banquet as my guest and rejoice thy heart ,

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: δείπνοισι τέρψω , τῆς δʼ Ἀθηναίων χθονὸς
sections.translation: and take thee to the Athenian land as a visitor forsooth , not as my own son . For I will not grieve my wife in her childless sorrow by my good fortune . But in time will I seize a happy moment

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: δάμαρτʼ ἐᾶν σε σκῆπτρα τἄμʼ ἔχειν χθονός .
sections.translation: and prevail on her to let thee wield my sceptre o ’ er the realm . Thy name shall be Ion , in accordance with what happened , for that thou wert the first to cross my path as I came forth from Apollo ’ s sanctuary . Go , gather every friend thou hast , and with them make merry o ’ er the flesh of sacrifice ,

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: πρόσειπε , μέλλων Δελφίδʼ ἐκλιπεῖν πόλιν .
sections.translation: on the eve of thy departure from the town of Delphi . On you , ye handmaids , silence I enjoin , for , if ye say one word to my wife , death awaits you .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν . ἓν δὲ τῆς τύχης ἄπεστί μοι ·
sections.translation: Well , I will go ; one thing my fortune lacks , for if I find not her that gave me birth , life is no life to me , my father ;

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἀβίωτον ἡμῖν . εἰ δʼ ἐπεύξασθαι χρεών ,
sections.translation: and , if I may make the prayer , Oh may that mother be a daughter of Athens ! that from-her I may inherit freedom of speech . For if a stranger settle in a city free from aliens , e ’ en though in name he be a citizen ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: δοῦλον πέπαται κοὐκ ἔχει παρρησίαν .
sections.translation: yet doth he find him-setf tongue-tied and debarred from open utterance .

sections.label: Section 1.676
sections.original: ὁρῶ δάκρυα καὶ πενθίμους
sections.translation: Weeping and lamentation and the beginning of mourning I foresee , when my mistress shall see her lord blest with a son ,

sections.label: Section 1.680
sections.original: αὐτὴ δʼ ἄπαις ᾖ καὶ λελειμμένη τέκνων .
sections.translation: while she is childless and forlorn . What was this oracle thou didst vouchsafe , prophetic son of Latona ? Whence came this boy , thy foster-child who lingers in thy temple ? who was his mother ?

sections.label: Section 1.685
sections.original: οὐ γάρ με σαίνει θέσφατα μή τινʼ ἔχῃ δόλον .
sections.translation: I like not thy oracle ; I fear there is some treachery . In terror I await the issue of this chance ;

sections.label: Section 1.690
sections.original: ἄτοπος ἄτοπα γὰρ δίδωσί μοι ,
sections.translation: for strange are these tidings and strange it is that the god declares them to me . There is guile connected with this waif ’ s fortune . All must allow that .

sections.label: Section 1.695
sections.original: — φίλαι , πότερʼ ἐμᾷ δεσποίνᾳ
sections.translation: Shall we , good friends , throw off disguise and tell our mistress this story about her husband in whom her all was centred and whose hopes , poor lady , she once shared ? But now in misery is she plunged , while he enjoys the smiles of fortune ;

sections.label: Section 1.700
sections.original: — πολιὸν ἐσπεσοῦσα γῆρας , πόσις δʼ ⋯
sections.translation: to hoary eld she drifteth fast , while he , her lord , pays no regard to his loved ones ,— the wretch , who came an alien to her house to share great wealth and failed to guard her fortunes ! Perdition catch this traitor to my lady !

sections.label: Section 1.705
sections.original: — καὶ θεοῖσιν μὴ τύχοι
sections.translation: never may he succeed in offering to the gods upon their blazing altar a hallowed cake with flames that augur well ! He shall know to his cost my regard

sections.label: Section 1.710
sections.original: τυραννίδος φίλα .
sections.translation: for my mistress . Now are sire and new-found son bent on the approaching feast .

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἰὼ δειράδες Παρνασοῦ πέτρας
sections.translation: Ho ! ye peaks of Parnassus

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: ἔχουσαι σκόπελον οὐράνιόν θʼ ἕδραν ,
sections.translation: that rear your rocky heads to heaven , where Bacchus with uplifted torch of blazing pine bounds nimbly amid his bacchanals , that range by night ! Never to my city come this boy !

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: νέαν δʼ ἁμέραν ἀπολιπὼν θάνοι .
sections.translation: let him die and leave his young life as it dawns ! For should our city fall on evil days , this bringing-in of strangers would supply it with a reason . Enough , enough for us Erechtheus ’ line that erst held sway !

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ὦ πρέσβυ παιδαγώγʼ Ἐρεχθέως πατρὸς
sections.translation: Aged retainer of my father Erechtheus while yet he lived and saw the light of day , mount to the god ’ s prophetic shrine that thou mayst share my gladness , if haply Loxias , great king , vouchsafe an answer touching my hopes of offspring ;

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: σὺν τοῖς φίλοις γὰρ ἡδὺ μὲν πράσσειν καλῶς ·
sections.translation: for sweet it is to share with friends prosperity , and sweet likewise to see a friendly face if any ill betide ,— which God forbid ! As thou of yore didst tend my sire , so now , thy mistress though I am , I take his place in tending thee .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ἄξιʼ ἀξίων γεννητόρων
sections.translation: Daughter , thy manners bear good witness still to thy noble lineage ; thou hast never brought shame upon those ancestors of thine , the children of the soil . A hand , I prithee , to the shrine ! a hand to lean upon !’ Tis a steep path thither , truly ;

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: συνεκπονοῦσα κῶλον ἰατρὸς γενοῦ .
sections.translation: but lend thy aid to guide my steps and make me young again .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: ἕπου νυν · ἴχνος δʼ ἐκφύλασσʼ ὅπου τίθης .
sections.translation: Come follow then , and look where thou art treading .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ἰδού .
sections.translation: Behold ! though my steps loiter , my thoughts take wings .

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: βάκτρῳ δʼ ἐρείδου περιφερῆ στίβον χθονός .
sections.translation: Lean on thy staff as thou climbest this winding path .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτο τυφλόν , ὅταν ἐγὼ βλέπω βραχύ .
sections.translation: Even this staff is a blind guide when I myself can scarcely see .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ὀρθῶς ἔλεξας · ἀλλὰ μὴ παρῇς κόπῳ .
sections.translation: True , but do not yield through fatigue .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: οὔκουν ἑκών γε · τοῦ δʼ ἀπόντος οὐ κρατῶ .
sections.translation: Never willingly , but I am not master of that which is mine no more .
